intro: Urostachys phlegmaria, also known as Phlegmariurus phlegmaria, is a type of plant in the Lycopodiaceae family, commonly referred to as a lycopod or club moss. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Urostachys phlegmaria include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Urostachys phlegmaria is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the production of teas and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for external applications.
